channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: More Sources of Power, Strike Against the Void, WoW Previews Midnight's Revelations Content Update
item.content:encoded:
In the next content update, take the next step in thwarting the forces of the Void—hunting down their leaders and uncovering new sources of power. Players will also face a new single-boss raid in Sporefall and Turbulent Timeways returns with Dragonflight dungeons and a new slate of rewards.

Get Ready for a Showdown

Facing the forces of the Void in Void Assaults and Ritual Sites isn’t enough to stop their machinations. To cut the threat off at its source, you’ll need to hunt down the leaders behind these brutal attacks on Azeroth. Track them through a portal in Voidstorm leading to two new locations:

  • Naigtal: A fungal, arcane-rich world now occupied by an insidious ethereal faction known as the Hal'hadar.
  • Val: An icy world once inhabited by the Legion where the Domanaar Imperator Pertinax has established his bastion of power.

The portal to these worlds is unstable and switches destinations every few days, bringing a rotating set of challenges and experiences. Players can undertake World Quests, defeat rare enemies, take part in events, and ultimately have a showdown with the leaders as world bosses.

Gain New Power with the Omnium Folio

Join Magister Umbric and Grand Magister Rommath on a mission to reconfigure and restore the Sunstrider Omnium. This ancient elven relic was originally created by Dath’remar Sunstrider to study and observe the various schools of magic and to serve as an academic repository. After years of silence, the Sunstrider Omnium has now reawakened, unsettling the Magisters and sowing disarray among them.

Once unlocked, you’ll be entrusted with the Omnium Folio, a runic ledger containing powerful runes of power you can use in battle. Continue taking part in weekly activities to imbue more runes—customizing these initial runes and unlocking their maximum potential to aid you in the fight against the Void.

New Raid: Sporefall

Sporefall is a single-boss raid located in Harandar, where you’ll face the fungal giant Rotmire. The raid will be available in Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties.

For this raid, we’re also trying something new for Mythic difficulty: flexible groups of 15–25 players.

Go Lorewalking with the Loa

Experience the lore of the loa with Assistant Lorewalker Li Li. You’ll dive deeper into the stories of the Zandalari, Drakkari, Gurubashi, and Darkspear trolls.

Help Loa Speaker Brek Hatch Jan’alai’s New Eggs

With the return of Jan’alai, Loa of Fire comes a return to past traditions. She’s ready to bring a new clutch of eggs into the world but Loa Speaker Brek requires assistance with the ceremony and with ensuring all the Loa’s young are released safely from their eggs. Be a part of welcoming the first of her offspring and protecting them as they take their first steps.

Begin the Next Chapter with Zul’jan

When the haranir call for a special gathering, Zul’jan attends as the Amani representative—only to be drawn into an ancient mystery surrounding the legacy of the Amani trolls. Join him and his uncle Kinduru on their journey of discovery to uncover the truth. This content will arrive after the release of the update.

Turbulent Timeways Returns

Turbulent Timeways returns with new rewards, including a new mount—Spawn of Vyranoth— and adds six Dragonflight dungeons to the rotation:

  • Algeth’ar Academy
  • Halls of Infusion
  • Neltharus
  • Ruby Life Pools
  • The Azure Vaults
  • Brackenhide Hollow

New Micro-Holiday: Darkspear Dash

Join the Darkspear trolls and their Zandalari allies as they race from Echo Isles to Silvermoon City for an in-game event inspired by the Running of the Trolls community event.  Prepare to run, leap, and spread rainbow cheer along the route.

More User Interface Updates

We’re adding additional quality-of-life updates to raid frames, damage meters, and more.

For more details on the content update, visit the PTR and review the forum notes.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/more-sources-of-power-strike-against-the-void-wow-previews-midnights-revelations-content-update-2000137954
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Greedfall Studio Spiders To Close Following Liquidation Confirmation
item.content:encoded:
The RPG space, and gaming as a whole, is losing yet another studio. Spiders, best known for GreedFall and its aptly-named follow-up GreedFall 2: The Dying World, has confirmed it is shutting down after entering liquidation.

The news follows earlier reporting from French outlet Origami, suggesting parent company Nacon had been unable to find a buyer for the Paris-based studio. Now, Spiders has made it official, stating that “after a long period without clear answers,” the team has received confirmation that the company is being dissolved.

Operations will cease immediately, raising questions about ongoing support, especially for Greedfall 2, which launched into early access in March. According to Spiders, one final piece of content is still on the way. Planned DLC for GreedFall 2 will be released through Nacon, though what happens after that is unclear. The studio has directed players to contact the publisher for any future support, effectively marking the end of direct developer involvement.

For fans of GreedFall and GreedFall 2, the news is especially bitter. Spiders built a reputation over the years as a mid-tier RPG specialist, delivering titles that often punched above their budget. With strong world-building and player choice, GreedFall found a dedicated audience thanks to its colonial fantasy setting and faction-driven storytelling.

For the gaming community as a whole, the closure is just the latest reminder of how volatile the gaming industry has become. Beloved studios, both large and small, seem constantly under siege by greed and profit margins, and Spiders probably won’t be the last studio to shutter this year.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/greedfall-studio-spiders-to-close-following-liquidation-confirmation-2000137951
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nightingale Gets Sidelined as Inflexion Games Reveals 16-Month Conan Exiles Remaster Commitment
item.content:encoded:
If you've been wondering why Nightingale has gone quiet, Inflection Games now has an answer for you: they've been deep in the Hyborian Age for over a year.

In a new Dev Bites filmed at audio director Neil Thompson's home (construction outside the studio made recording impossible), CEO Aaryn Flynn confirmed Inflexion studio has spent roughly 16 months as the development partner on Conan Exiles Enhanced, the free UE5 remaster of Funcom's survival game launching May 5. The scope of that work was significant: over 10,000 art assets updated to UE5 physical-based rendering standards, hundreds of bug fixes, dozens of new and improved UI screens, and a complete rework of the game's lighting across every dungeon and environment.

The implication for Nightingale fans isn't great. Flynn was straightforward about it: small patches and quality-of-life updates will keep coming as the design team brings ideas forward, but meaningful content expansions, new systems, or any serious roadmap commitment are off the table for the foreseeable future. Inflexion still has more Conan Exiles work planned through 2026.

Thompson offered some silver lining, noting the UE5 systems, surface materials, and lighting pipelines Inflection built while developing Nightingale fed directly into the remaster work. Flynn expressed hope those lessons will eventually flow back the other direction.

Both developers were candid that the financial reality of the games industry right now made this kind of work essential. "Nightingale is our baby," Flynn said, and the team clearly means it. The question is just how long fans will be waiting.

Conan Exiles Enhanced launches as a free Steam upgrade on May 5, 2026. As for Nightingale we're going to have to keep our eyes peeled on where the game will go once the team is finished with their Exiles work.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/nightingale-gets-sidelined-as-inflexion-games-reveals-16-month-conan-exiles-remaster-commitment-2000137943
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: After Two Decades, Text-Based MMORPG TORN Surprisingly Breaks the Six-Figure Daily Player Mark
item.content:encoded:
Extremely oldschool text-based MMO TORN has hit an enormous milestone. The browser-based crime MMORPG that has been running since 2004, has hit a whopping 100,000 daily active players - a feat the founder himself once believed to be impossible.

In the days of game engines that trivialize how visually appealing games can be, it's always a surprise to hear a longstanding text-based MMO is not only still running, but thriving. TORN crossed that threshold of 100k daily active players on April 27, marking the first time in its 22-year history that it has reached six figures for daily logins. To put that in context, TORN recorded 48,515 daily active players in January 2024. It more than doubled that number in just over two years.

Founder Joe Chedburn attributed the growth to two parallel efforts: infrastructure optimization to handle the increased load, and expanded advertising and PR campaigns. "Breaking the five-digit barrier on the login page counter has been a dream of mine for a very long time, one I genuinely never thought was possible," Chedburn said in the announcement.

TORN launched in 2004 as a PHP project built by a 16-year-old Chedburn working part-time at a bakery chain. The game now runs with a team of around 50 developers spread across the UK, USA, Ukraine, Thailand, and across the world. The team states that their growth has been exponential, hitting 30,000 new players in the past year alone, arriving alongside a veteran playerbase that has been with the game for decades.

Despite Torn being just one of several text-based MMOs, the game continues to operate without shareholders utilizing a freemium model to keep players invested, with updates shipping only when the team considers them ready, and fanfests actively taking place to bring players together. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/after-two-decades-text-based-mmorpg-torn-surprisingly-breaks-the-six-figure-daily-player-mark-2000137942
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Runescape's Big Player Avatar And Dungeoneering Revamps Are Penned Into Its May 2026 Calendar
item.content:encoded:
Two major new features are coming to the modern Runescape game in May, with one many years in the making, as Jagex has announced what’s to come via May’s Month Ahead preview. This preview blog goes week-by-week to show off what’s planned throughout the month, including both updates and blogs of what’s coming. 

The two big hitters are the “Dungeoneering Remastered” revamp, announced back in March, and the start of the long-in-the-works Player Avatar system. 

As previously discussed by Jagex, the Dungeoneering rework is titled with a “Remastered” label because they’re aiming on ““improving the skill's gameplay, balance and mechanics without changing the core identity of the skill,” making it more intuitive and approachable. While it’s not core to the Remaster, there’ll also be some visual tweaks to bring it up to date. 

The Dungeoneering Remastered update arrives the week of May 17, alongside a Community Topic blog that discusses it and a Powers of the Occult Market Drop. 

Players will also get the first leg of the Player Avatar revamp. The initiative was initially announced back in 2021, but the team only really picked progress back up recently. The team recently gave an “under the hood” look back in March, and now, it seems they’re ready to give it an initial whirl. 

A few days ago, the team gave a more extensive look at what’s to come from these changes. At this launch, we’ll see more new and refreshed old outfits, refreshed hairstyles and bears, and replacements of avatar heads and chatheads. The blog goes into some more tech detail about the complexities of how clothes work on custom models. 

These aren’t the only updates in Runescape shown off in the May blog. This week, there was already a major visual overhaul for the world and combat, plus some decluttering efforts. Then, next week, there’ll be a limited-time event, as well as a beta test for the Player Avatar launch. Finally, the week of May 18 sees more of a focus on optimization with some graphical rendering improvements, with a blog about it arriving the week of May 4.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/runescapes-big-player-avatar-and-dungeoneering-revamps-are-penned-into-its-may-2026-calendar-2000137950
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
